name: U.S. National Security Architecture — Senior Leadership KPV Assessment
description: KPV assessment of the U.S. national security senior leadership architecture. All roles are public-record positions. AO scores reflect observability to foreign adversarial intelligence. Sources: NSC charter, public biographies, CRS reports, open government directories.

roles.title: National Security Advisor
roles.notes: Highest relationship-capital KPV in U.S. government. Presidential access, allied NSA relationships, IC coordination role. Tacit knowledge of inter-agency equities irreplaceable. Source: CRS R45607, public NSC charter.

roles.title: Director of National Intelligence
roles.notes: IC integration role. Relationship capital with Five Eyes partners' intelligence chiefs is non-transferable. Statutory authority under IRTPA. Source: IRTPA 2004; ODNI public org chart.

roles.title: Director, Central Intelligence Agency
roles.notes: HUMINT network relationships and covert programme oversight. Cover and source relationships are substantially tacit. Source: CIA public org chart; CRS IC oversight reports.

roles.title: Secretary of Defense
roles.notes: NATO ministerial relationships; congressional defense committee relationships. Higher documentation ratio than civilian IC roles due to statutory/regulatory framework. Source: DoD org; 10 U.S.C.

roles.title: Chairman, Joint Chiefs of Staff
roles.notes: Senior military advisor; allied CHOD relationships built over career. Succession path exists (VCJCS) but relationship capital not transferable. Source: 10 U.S.C. § 151; public DoD directory.

roles.title: Director, National Security Agency / CYBERCOM
roles.notes: Dual-hatted; SIGINT and CYBERCOM integration. Five Eyes SIGINT partnership relationships substantially tacit. Source: NSA/CSS public charter; CRS R43955.

roles.title: Director, Defense Intelligence Agency
roles.notes: Defense HUMINT and all-source analysis. Allied defence intelligence relationships. Source: DIA public org; CRS R44681.

roles.title: FBI Executive Assistant Director, National Security
roles.notes: Counterintelligence programme oversight. Source protection and CI relationship capital. Source: FBI public org; HPSCI reports.

roles.title: NSC Coordinator for Intelligence and Defense
roles.notes: Inter-agency cyber coordination; classified network access to all IC products. Tacit knowledge of inter-agency equities and allied cyber agreements. Source: NSC public charter; EO 13800.

roles.title: Commander, U.S. Strategic Command
roles.notes: Nuclear operations authority; allied extended deterrence relationships; ICBM/SLBM/bomber force command experience. KPCI elevated by tacit nuclear command relationships. Source: STRATCOM public charter; 10 U.S.C. § 161.

roles.title: USD (Research & Engineering) — Critical Technologies
roles.notes: DIB technology transition authority; CHIPS/CICP-adjacent programme oversight. Source: 10 U.S.C. § 4001; USD(R&E) public org.

roles.title: Director, DARPA
roles.notes: Programme manager network relationships; technology horizon knowledge. DARPA PM network is itself a distributed KPV — individual PMs hold domain-specific tacit knowledge. Source: DARPA public org.

roles.title: DoD DPA Title III Programme Director
roles.notes: Title III investment authority relationships with domestic processing sector; CICP co-investment programme knowledge. Key person in CICP implementation architecture. Source: 50 U.S.C. § 4533; DPA Title III public programme descriptions.

roles.title: Commander, U.S. Cyber Command
roles.notes: Cyber operations authorities; Five Eyes cyber coordination. Source: CYBERCOM public charter; EO 13800.

roles.title: Commander, Space Command
roles.notes: GPS/PNT architecture oversight; space domain awareness relationships. Source: SPACECOM public charter.

roles.title: Director, CISA
roles.notes: Critical infrastructure protection coordination; private sector trust relationships. Source: CISA Enabling Act 2018; CISA public org.

roles.title: Secretary of State — National Security Equities
roles.notes: Allied foreign minister relationships; arms control negotiation authority. Richard Lugar precedent: relationship capital non-transferable. Source: State Dept public org; CRS.

roles.title: Director, National Clandestine Service
roles.notes: HUMINT network oversight; source protection relationships. Highest tacit knowledge concentration in the IC. Source: CIA public org (limited; by design).

roles.title: Director, Science & Technology (CIA)
roles.notes: Technical intelligence collection capability. Source: CIA public org.

roles.title: Deputy Director for Analysis (CIA)
roles.notes: All-source analysis product leadership. Source: CIA public org.

roles.title: Critical Minerals / CICP Programme Manager
roles.notes: DPA Title III rare earth processing programme management. Lynas, MP Materials co-investment relationships. CICP Tier-2 KPV. Source: DoD DPA Title III public contract awards.

roles.title: FBI Counterintelligence Division — China Programme
roles.notes: Thousand Talents investigation programme knowledge. Source protection for CI sources. Source: FBI public CI programme descriptions; CRS R46749.

roles.title: FBI WMD Directorate
roles.notes: Nuclear/CBRN counterproliferation programme. Source: FBI public WMD Directorate description.
